title: Food allergy and diet register
summary: Practical guidance for “Food allergy and diet register” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.
content:
Practical guidance for “Food allergy and diet register” within “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”. It supports safe storage, temperature control, preparation, rationing, and spoilage decisions, produces a visible result, and states when to stop or move to a safer fallback.

Start with: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop condition: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Reduced fallback: If “Food allergy and diet register” cannot be completed, do only the first verified step, mark the remaining capability unavailable, and use a shelf-stable meal prepared with safe water and clean utensils.

steps: Use a written trigger, one accountable owner, a visible result, and a scheduled review for this exact task. This is the opening check for “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Identify crop or animal needs, season, soil, water quality, exposure, labour, and the smallest useful trial. Record the result under “Food · Agriculture · Preservation” before continuing “Food allergy and diet register”.

Keep clean stock, seed, feed, harvest, compost, manure, chemicals, and diseased material separated and labelled. Recheck this point before closing “Food allergy and diet register” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Use observation rows or small test plots before changing the whole system. Treat any uncertainty here as an unresolved item in “Food allergy and diet register” for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Record weather, watering, inputs, pests, disease signs, growth, yield, losses, and labour. A second person should be able to verify this part of “Food allergy and diet register”.

Save diverse healthy seed or breeding stock only from known sources and revise the next cycle from measured results. Include the outcome of “Food allergy and diet register” in the handover for “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
warnings: Do not use unknown plants, contaminated water, untreated sewage, unidentified chemicals, or diseased material in food production. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
Avoid changing every plot, seed lot, feed source, or animal group at once. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

failure_signs: Stop and reassess “Food allergy and diet register” when growth, yield, water use, soil condition, pest pressure, disease, or animal welfare worsens after the change. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

Stop and reassess “Food allergy and diet register” when records cannot distinguish the trial from the control or the source of seed, feed, water, and inputs becomes uncertain. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue “Food allergy and diet register” while temperature history, allergen status, flood contact, packaging integrity, or the origin of the food cannot be established. Apply this boundary to “Food allergy and diet register” in “Food · Agriculture · Preservation”.

This card is not authorization to taste questionable food to test it, hide spoilage with cooking, or serve an unknown ingredient to someone with a serious allergy.
